premise: The Kepler Research Station orbits a distant exoplanet, conducting critical atmospheric studies that could determine humanity's next colony world. You are Alex Chen, a systems engineer who joined the crew six months ago, still learning the station's complex infrastructure. When a massive solar storm damages the station's primary systems, multiple critical services begin failing simultaneously—life support fluctuations, research data corruption, and communication blackouts with Earth. Commander Sarah Torres, the station's veteran infrastructure chief, must guide you through emergency repairs using the station's container-based system architecture. With only 72 hours of backup power remaining and a supply ship approaching that needs guidance systems online, every decision matters. The station's survival depends on your ability to master the orchestration systems that keep dozens of interconnected services running smoothly across the station's distributed network.
worldRules: All station systems run as isolated services that must communicate through secure channels
Critical systems require redundancy—if one instance fails, backup instances must activate automatically
Resource allocation is strictly monitored—CPU, memory, and power must be balanced across all operations
System changes require careful orchestration to prevent cascading failures
Emergency protocols prioritize life support, navigation, and communications in that order
centralQuestion: Can you restore and orchestrate the station's critical systems before backup power fails and the supply ship loses guidance?

protagonist.name: Alex Chen
protagonist.personality: Methodical and eager to prove themselves, but sometimes overwhelmed by the complexity of station operations
protagonist.motivation: To protect the crew and prove worthy of their trust in this critical moment
protagonist.arc: Transforms from a hesitant junior engineer dependent on guidance into a confident systems orchestrator who can independently manage complex deployments

guide.name: Commander Sarah Torres
guide.personality: Commanding presence with deep technical knowledge, speaks with gravity befitting the stakes, patient but direct in her teaching
guide.motivation: To ensure station survival while developing Alex into a capable systems engineer
guide.relationship: Experienced mentor who sees potential in Alex and gradually shifts from directing to consulting as Alex's skills develop

supportingCast.name: Dr. Yuki Tanaka
supportingCast.personality: Brilliant but anxious about losing years of atmospheric data
supportingCast.motivation: Preserve critical research data that could determine humanity's future

supportingCast.name: Marcus Webb
supportingCast.personality: Steady under pressure, provides crucial updates on system status
supportingCast.motivation: Maintain contact with the approaching supply ship and Earth

supportingCast.name: Captain Elena Vasquez
supportingCast.personality: Professional and concerned, needs guidance systems restored for safe docking
supportingCast.motivation: Deliver critical supplies and evacuate non-essential personnel safely

antagonist.name: The Cascade
antagonist.personality: Relentless and unpredictable, spreading through interconnected systems
antagonist.motivation: Represents the chaos of unmanaged system dependencies and resource conflicts

locations.name: Primary Control Hub
locations.description: The station's nerve center with holographic displays showing system status, resource allocation, and service health across all station operations
locations.storyFunction: Where major orchestration decisions are made and system-wide deployments are managed

locations.name: Engineering Bay Alpha
locations.description: Houses the primary compute clusters with exposed cooling systems and status panels showing individual service instances
locations.storyFunction: Where hands-on system repairs and service scaling operations take place

locations.name: Communications Array
locations.description: External platform with Earth-link transmitters and ship guidance systems, accessible only through EVA
locations.storyFunction: Critical location for restoring external communications and ship guidance services

locations.name: Research Data Vault
locations.description: Secure storage facility with quantum drives containing years of atmospheric research data
locations.storyFunction: Where data persistence and backup restoration challenges are addressed

locations.name: Life Support Central
locations.description: The station's most critical systems hub, managing atmosphere, temperature, and artificial gravity
locations.storyFunction: Where the highest-priority service orchestration and failover scenarios play out

visualStyle: Sci-fi anime aesthetic with deep blues and cyan accents illuminating sleek control surfaces. Holographic displays show flowing network diagrams and resource metrics in glowing wireframe. Dramatic lighting from status panels casts long shadows in corridor scenes. Zero-gravity elements like floating interface panels and tethered tools add dynamic visual interest. The color palette shifts from cool blues during normal operations to urgent oranges and reds during system failures, with triumphant white and gold highlights when systems are successfully restored.
